title: triticum vulgare convar. muticum
intro: Triticum vulgare convar. muticum, also known as wild rye, is a subspecies of the common wheat plant that belongs to the Triticeae tribe of grasses. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Triticum vulgare convar. muticum include reducing inflammation and improving digestive health, as well as supporting immune function and reducing stress. The plant can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, often used to promote relaxation and soothe digestive issues.
